title: All about the Trade-in Deferred Payout
bodyHtml:
As refurbishers, you receive customers’ trade-in devices before paying for them. Therefore, Back Market must take specific precautions to protect Trade-in customers against failed and refused payments.

 

Trade-in Deferred Payout is one way we protect customers — it also helps you run and grow your Trade-in activities while having lower Sales activities. 

 

Learn more about our processes to protect Trade-in customers’ payments

 

What is the Trade-in Deferred Payout?

The Trade-in Deferred Payout is additional to your Deferred Payout (Sales) and is separate from the amount retained based on your sales volume.

 

Also known as a “Trade-in Security Payment”, it aims to retain money from your payout to guarantee the payment of your Trade-in orders.

 

Learn more about Sale deferred payouts

 

How is your Trade-in Deferred Payout computed? 

The amount of your Trade-in Deferred Payout is calculated based on your:

 

  • Trade-in Exposure
  • Tiering (useful for calculating your Deferred Payout linked to Sales activities)
  • Wallet balance, as disclosed in your Back Office
  • Trade-in history (including any past failed payments)

 

If your Trade-in and Sales activities are well balanced (i.e. not generating risks for customers’ payments), your Trade-in Deferred Payout will equal 0.

 

If applicable, the Trade-in Deferred Payout is retained and reevaluated at each payout date. The Trade-in Deferred Payout amount can increase gradually based on your Trade-in Exposure, but will not exceed your Trade-in Exposure. Where a Trade-in Deferred Payout applies on top of a sales deferred payout, Back Market still guarantees 85% of each payout (compared to 95% when only a sales deferred payout is applied). The maximum amount retained at each payout to build to the Trade-in Deferred Payout also will not exceed €15,000.

 

Note that in case of suspension or limitation on sales or Trade-in, as well as in case of an increase of your Deferred Payout (Sales), your Trade-in Deferred Payout will be adjusted up to 50% of your payout (for Trade-in Deferred Payout only, notwithstanding any additional Sales Deferred Payout). 

 

How to track your Trade-in Deferred Payout?

Our team is working to ensure that the Trade-in Deferred Payout amount is visible and easily identifiable separately from your Deferred Payout (Sales). This would be displayed on both your invoice and in your Back Office. This feature will be available as soon as possible.

 

Until then, note that the Deferred Payout amount displayed on your invoices and in your Back Office includes both your Sales and your Trade-in Deferred Payout. 


When may your Trade-in Deferred Payout be used?

Your Trade-in Deferred Payout will only be used to pay your Trade-in customers if:

 

  • You have failed payments
  • Neither your Sales nor a wire transfer can cover the debt generated by your failed payments

 

When will my Trade-in Deferred Payout be released?

At each payout, your Trade-in Deferred Payout is reassessed and compared to your Trade-in Exposure. Any euro withheld above your Trade-in Exposure is released instantly.

 

If you exit the marketplace, your Trade-in Deferred Payout will be released within a maximum of 60 days of your exit, provided that you complied with your payment obligations to your Trade-in customers.

 

How can I reduce my Trade-in Deferred Payout?

The only way is to boost your Sales on Back Market. By doing so, your Trade-in Exposure will decrease and therefore your Trade-in Deferred Payout will also.
